mock_now_execution: Approve the current simulator-backed foundation only. Keep live publication, live-provider readiness, and production signoff outside the approved claim.
actual_production_strategy_later: Extend the same tuple, parity, shell, and evidence contracts into later live-provider and live-channel onboarding without redefining Phase 0 semantics.

mandatorySuites.label: Domain transition and event schema compatibility
mandatorySuites.summary: Canonical transition and schema compatibility remain machine-verified with bounded, explicit catalog gaps rather than silent drift.

mandatorySuites.label: Route intent, freshness, and scoped mutation gate
mandatorySuites.summary: Route-intent, projection freshness, and scoped mutation proof are current, with the remaining browser specimen gaps held as explicit guarded rows.

mandatorySuites.label: Adapter replay, duplicate, quarantine, and fallback review
mandatorySuites.summary: Replay, duplicate clustering, quarantine, and fallback review are explicitly proven, with zero duplicate side effects and one bounded event-catalog gap kept visible.

mandatorySuites.label: Shell accessibility, preview environment, and smoke proof
mandatorySuites.summary: All shell families are exercised, but every smoke verdict remains rightly withheld because publication parity and browser truth are not yet exact.

mandatorySuites.label: Release freeze, restore, and non-production canary rehearsals
mandatorySuites.summary: Release-watch, canary, rollback, and restore proof is exact for the simulator-first baseline, with no premature applied success and no live control reopening.

contradictionChecks.summary: Surface authority and shell smoke both keep live browser publication withheld.
Release rehearsals do not reopen live control during the simulator-first gate.
The earlier exit pack withheld the claim, and this gate now resolves that ambiguity by approving only the simulator-first foundation baseline.

gateQuestions.question: Are all mandatory Phase 0 tasks complete and traceable?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. Every task before seq_138 is complete in the checklist and repository traceability still reports zero requirement gaps.

gateQuestions.question: Did verification suites 133-137 pass with machine-readable evidence?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. The suites passed, while seq_136 and seq_137 still correctly keep live publication and live control withheld.

gateQuestions.question: Are the canonical shell, runtime, publication, accessibility, replay, and recovery invariants demonstrably in place?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes for the simulator-first baseline. The invariants are implemented and machine-tested, but live surface calm/writable truth is still intentionally withheld.

gateQuestions.question: Are simulator-first external dependency assumptions explicit and bounded?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. Adapter validation, handover matrices, and deferred live-provider items are explicit, bounded, and fail closed.

gateQuestions.question: Which items are genuinely deferred to later live-provider or live-channel phases, and why are they not Phase 0 blockers?
gateQuestions.answer: They are listed explicitly in the open-items register and are non-blocking because this gate approves only simulator-first foundation readiness.

gateQuestions.question: Is there any unresolved contradiction between the canonical docs, prompts, and implemented foundation artifacts?
gateQuestions.answer: No current contradiction was detected. The remaining constraints all preserve fail-closed truth instead of weakening the blueprints.

assumptions.summary: Validated outputs from tasks 001-137 remain the governing machine-readable baseline for this gate pack.

risks.summary: Phase 0 ends with live surface truth withheld; later phases must not misread this gate as permission to advertise live calm/writable posture.
Deferred provider/channel work must stay explicit so later delivery does not launder simulator-first proof into live-provider claims.
